meta.title: WGLT Financial Literacy Simulator - 48 Week Master Data
meta.structure: 48 weeks, 3 scenarios per week, 3 choices per scenario
notes: Each week should randomly select one of the three scenarios and keep that selection locked for the current playthrough.
Choices update immediate budget values and delayed consequence flags.
Use delayed consequence checks during weekly flow and 4-week review checkpoints.

weeks.learningGoal: Create a financial plan before spending money

scenarios.title: Payday Temptation
scenarios.prompt: You get your first paycheck and want to spend it right away before locking your year plan.

choices.label: Set a rough plan then review later
choices.summary: Makes a decent start but leaves room for confusion.

scenarios.title: Mall Invite
scenarios.prompt: A cousin invites you to the mall on payday, but your plan is not locked yet.

choices.label: Go but promise not to buy much
choices.summary: Tries to balance fun and structure.

scenarios.title: Online Deal
scenarios.prompt: A limited-time sale pops up on your phone before you finish your budget.

choices.label: Wait until the plan is done
choices.summary: Protects the budget from impulse pressure.

choices.label: Save the item for later
choices.summary: Delays the decision without losing it.

choices.label: Buy it now because it is on sale

weeks.learningGoal: Understand the difference between needs and wants

scenarios.title: Toiletries vs Treat
scenarios.prompt: You need toiletries, but snacks and a drink sound fun after school.

choices.label: Split money between both
choices.summary: Tries balance but weakens the plan.

choices.label: Spend on snacks and skip the need

scenarios.title: Supplies vs Gift Card
scenarios.prompt: You need school supplies, but a game card is calling your name.

choices.label: Buy the supplies and a tiny treat

scenarios.title: Lunch Money Choice
scenarios.prompt: You have just enough for either lunch money for two days or a fun want today.

choices.label: Reduce the lunch plan a little
Spend it on the want
choices.summary: Creates avoidable pressure later in the week.

weeks.learningGoal: Learn to save money first (pay yourself first)

scenarios.title: First Savings Transfer
scenarios.prompt: You get paid and can move money to savings immediately.

choices.label: Save a smaller amount
choices.summary: Still builds the habit, but slower.

scenarios.title: Advice at Home
scenarios.prompt: Someone at home tells you to save first before spending on fun.

choices.label: Ignore the advice
choices.summary: Makes saving too easy to delay.

scenarios.title: Sale Day Conflict
scenarios.prompt: A sale pops up on the same day you planned to save.

choices.label: Save first and skip the sale
Spend instead of saving
choices.summary: Trades future security for today’s feeling.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand how small spending decisions add up

scenarios.title: After-School Snacks
scenarios.prompt: You keep stopping for small snacks after school this week.

choices.label: Keep buying a little every day

scenarios.title: Cheap Fun Trap
scenarios.prompt: You say yes to a few cheap buys because each one feels small.

choices.label: Keep spending because it is only a little

scenarios.title: Convenience Habit
scenarios.prompt: You pay extra for convenience several times in one week.

choices.label: Keep choosing convenience
choices.summary: Builds a leak that shows later.

weeks.learningGoal: Recognize how daily food spending impacts a budget

scenarios.title: Forgot Lunch
scenarios.prompt: You forgot your lunch and have to decide what to do at school.

choices.label: Buy a small option
choices.summary: Controls hunger without wrecking the budget.

choices.label: Buy a full meal
choices.summary: Solves the problem but costs more.

scenarios.title: Convenience Store Stop
scenarios.prompt: Friends stop at a store after school for snacks and drinks.

choices.label: Buy one low-cost item
choices.summary: Sets a boundary while joining in.

choices.label: Match what everyone else buys
choices.summary: Lets the group set your spending.

scenarios.title: Post-Practice Hunger
scenarios.prompt: You are hungry after practice and pass a fast-food place.

choices.label: Buy a combo and dessert
choices.summary: Turns a simple stop into a budget hit.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand peer pressure and spending choices

scenarios.title: Pizza Night
scenarios.prompt: Friends want pizza tonight and ask you to join.

choices.label: Go only if it was already planned
Go because everyone else is going

scenarios.title: Team Snack Fund
scenarios.prompt: Your group wants everyone to chip in for snacks.

choices.label: Contribute only if it fits the budget
Pay more than you planned to fit in

scenarios.title: Movie Invite
scenarios.prompt: Friends want to see a movie this weekend.

choices.label: Suggest a cheaper hangout
choices.summary: Keeps the social connection without overspending.

choices.label: Go but set a spending cap
Say yes and spend freely
choices.summary: Trades the plan for the moment.

weeks.learningGoal: Track spending and stay within a budget plan

scenarios.title: Receipt Check
scenarios.prompt: You have receipts from the week and need to decide whether to review them.

choices.label: Ignore the receipts
choices.summary: Makes next week harder to improve.

scenarios.title: Budget App Entry
scenarios.prompt: You can log your spending today or tell yourself you will remember it.

choices.label: Log most of it later tonight

scenarios.title: Cash Left Question
scenarios.prompt: You are not sure where some of your money went this week.

choices.label: Pause and reconcile the budget
choices.summary: Finds the leak before it grows.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand how small unexpected expenses affect savings

scenarios.title: Lost Charger
scenarios.prompt: You misplaced your charger and need a replacement soon.

scenarios.title: Club Fee Reminder
scenarios.prompt: A small school fee appears with short notice.

choices.label: Use savings or flexible money carefully
Ask for a little more time
Ignore it until it gets worse

scenarios.title: Broken Headphones
scenarios.prompt: Your headphones stop working right before you need them.

choices.label: Choose a low-cost fix or replacement
Use a temporary workaround
choices.summary: Saves money but may inconvenience you.

weeks.learningGoal: Learn the difference between checking and savings accounts

scenarios.title: Cash or Deposit
scenarios.prompt: You receive money and need to decide whether to carry it or deposit it.

choices.label: Deposit it into the right account
Keep a little cash and deposit the rest
Keep it all on you
choices.summary: Raises the risk of spending or losing it.

scenarios.title: Checking vs Savings
scenarios.prompt: You have money that does not need to be spent this week.

choices.label: Leave it in checking for now
Spend it because it is easy to access
choices.summary: Confuses available money with free money.

scenarios.title: Birthday Money Choice
scenarios.prompt: You get birthday money and can either protect it or burn through it.

choices.label: Save most of it
choices.summary: Uses a gift to build stability.

choices.label: Split it between saving and fun

weeks.learningGoal: Understand delayed gratification

scenarios.title: Shoes Now or Later
scenarios.prompt: You want new shoes but also have a bigger goal later.

choices.label: Wait and keep saving
choices.summary: Uses patience to win bigger later.

scenarios.title: Accessories vs Goal
scenarios.prompt: Small upgrades tempt you while you are saving for something larger.

choices.label: Stay focused on the larger goal
Buy several extras now
choices.summary: Lets the little wins block the bigger one.

scenarios.title: Concert Ticket Temptation
scenarios.prompt: A fun event appears while you are trying to build savings.

choices.label: Skip it and protect the goal
Look for a cheaper option
choices.summary: Tries to enjoy life without losing the plan.

weeks.learningGoal: Learn how income affects budgeting decisions

scenarios.title: Extra Shift Offer
scenarios.prompt: You are offered extra work hours this week.

choices.label: Take the hours and plan the money
Take some extra time but not all
Skip extra income and still spend big
choices.summary: Weakens the budget from both sides.

scenarios.title: Smaller Paycheck
scenarios.prompt: Your paycheck is lower than expected this week.

choices.label: Make only a small adjustment
choices.summary: Helps some, but may still squeeze the budget.

choices.label: Keep spending like nothing changed
choices.summary: Pretends the numbers do not matter.

scenarios.title: Side Hustle Idea
scenarios.prompt: You have a chance to earn a little extra money.

choices.label: Use the extra income for goals and savings
choices.summary: Gives the new money a job.

choices.label: Use part for fun and part for goals
Spend all extra money right away
choices.summary: Lets lifestyle grow faster than purpose.

weeks.learningGoal: See how good financial habits build stability

scenarios.title: Strong Week
scenarios.prompt: You stayed close to your budget and have money left over.

choices.label: Save the extra money
choices.summary: Turns one good week into a habit.

choices.label: Use a little and save a little
Spend it because you earned it

scenarios.title: Reset Moment
scenarios.prompt: You had a messy week and can choose how to respond.

choices.label: Ignore it and hope next week is better

scenarios.title: Routine Check
scenarios.prompt: You notice your money choices are becoming automatic.

choices.label: Keep most habits but loosen one
Drop the routine because you feel comfortable

weeks.learningGoal: Understand recurring expenses

scenarios.title: Phone Bill
scenarios.prompt: A regular phone bill is due again.

choices.label: Pay it from planned money
choices.summary: Shows recurring costs belong in the budget.

choices.label: Pay it late in the week

scenarios.title: Transportation Cost
scenarios.prompt: A recurring transport cost comes up again this week.

choices.label: Cut another category to cover it
choices.summary: Creates a patch, not a plan.

choices.label: Ignore it and hope it works out

scenarios.title: Monthly Service
scenarios.prompt: A regular service payment needs attention.

choices.label: Keep it planned and on time
choices.summary: Matches recurring expense with recurring preparation.

choices.label: Forget it and let it pile up

weeks.learningGoal: Learn why paying bills on time matters

scenarios.title: Due Date Today
scenarios.prompt: A bill is due today and you have the money ready.

choices.label: Pay it later this week
choices.summary: May still work, but adds risk.

scenarios.title: Reminder Notice
scenarios.prompt: You get a reminder that payment is due soon.

choices.label: Set a calendar alert and wait
Dismiss the reminder
choices.summary: Turns a warning into a problem.

scenarios.title: Bill Money Temptation
scenarios.prompt: You are tempted to use money set aside for a bill.

choices.label: Borrow a little and replace it fast

weeks.learningGoal: Recognize how subscriptions add up

scenarios.title: Streaming Stack
scenarios.prompt: You notice you are paying for more than one subscription.

choices.label: Cancel what you do not use
Keep all of them because it is convenient

scenarios.title: Trial Period Ending
scenarios.prompt: A free trial is about to become a paid subscription.

choices.label: Cancel before the charge hits
choices.summary: Reads ahead and acts on time.

choices.label: Keep it only if it truly fits the budget
Forget about it and let it charge

scenarios.title: Music Plan Upgrade
scenarios.prompt: You are offered a premium plan with extras you do not really need.

choices.label: Stay with the basic or free plan
Try it for one month with a reminder
Upgrade without checking the cost over time
choices.summary: Lets a small upgrade become a recurring leak.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand financial responsibility and future choices

scenarios.title: Shared Item Care
scenarios.prompt: You borrowed something and damaged it by accident.

choices.label: Own it and make a plan to fix it
Explain it and ask for time

scenarios.title: Missed Preparation
scenarios.prompt: You failed to plan for a known cost and now have to adjust.

choices.label: Fix the budget and learn from it
choices.summary: Uses the mistake as a teacher.

choices.label: Patch the problem for now
choices.summary: Gets through, but without a deep fix.

choices.label: Blame the situation and do nothing

scenarios.title: Future Option
scenarios.prompt: A chance later in the month depends on what you do right now.

weeks.learningGoal: Learn how sharing costs with friends affects budgets

scenarios.title: Split the Ride
scenarios.prompt: Friends want to split a ride cost for an event.

choices.label: Join only if it fits the budget
choices.summary: Keeps social life connected to the plan.

choices.label: Pay without checking your budget
choices.summary: Lets the group decide for you.

scenarios.title: Group Gift
scenarios.prompt: A group gift is being collected for someone.

choices.label: Contribute an amount you can afford
Contribute a small amount and explain
Contribute more than planned to look good

scenarios.title: Shared Food Cost
scenarios.prompt: Everyone wants to split a food order evenly even though you got less.

choices.label: Pay this time but note it
choices.summary: Mixed, because it avoids conflict but costs you.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand impulse buying

scenarios.title: Store Display
scenarios.prompt: A flashy item near checkout catches your eye.

choices.label: Leave it and stick to the list

scenarios.title: Online Cart
scenarios.prompt: Something in your cart feels fun, but not necessary.

choices.label: Save it to a wish list
choices.summary: Keeps the option without the instant spend.

choices.label: Check out immediately
choices.summary: Turns a feeling into a cost.

scenarios.title: Limited Time Sign
scenarios.prompt: A timer says the deal ends soon.

choices.label: Ignore the pressure and decide calmly
choices.summary: Sees urgency as a tactic, not a command.
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

choices.label: Research quickly before deciding
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

choices.label: Buy because the timer is ticking
choices.summary: Treats urgency like proof of value.

tracksTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

weeks.learningGoal: Understand unexpected life events and budgeting

scenarios.title: Schedule Change
scenarios.prompt: A surprise schedule change creates an extra cost this week.

choices.label: Adjust the budget and cover it calmly
Act like it did not happen until later

scenarios.title: School Cost Surprise
scenarios.prompt: A last-minute school-related expense appears.

choices.label: Use planned backup money or savings

scenarios.title: Family Schedule Shift
scenarios.prompt: A change at home affects transportation or meals.

choices.label: Panic spend for convenience
choices.summary: Makes a hard week more expensive.

weeks.learningGoal: Learn the importance of emergency funds

scenarios.title: Small Emergency
scenarios.prompt: A real but manageable surprise cost hits.

choices.label: Use flexible spending and rebuild later
Use money that was meant for something critical
choices.summary: Solves one problem by creating another.

scenarios.title: Need It Fast
scenarios.prompt: You need money quickly for an unexpected issue.

choices.label: Ask for help, then make a payback plan
Charge ahead without a plan
choices.summary: Quick fixes can cost more later.

scenarios.title: After the Emergency
scenarios.prompt: The problem is handled, but now you choose what to do next.

choices.label: Rebuild the fund
choices.summary: Turns one good move into a long-term system.

choices.label: Forget rebuilding it
choices.summary: Leaves the next surprise waiting empty-handed.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand helping family vs protecting your budget

scenarios.title: Family Request
scenarios.prompt: A family member needs a little financial help.

choices.label: Help a smaller amount and explain
Give more than you can handle
choices.summary: Kind heart, but risky money choice.

scenarios.title: Shared Responsibility
scenarios.prompt: A home-related cost is discussed and you want to contribute.

choices.label: Contribute what you planned for
choices.summary: Supports the home without harming yourself.

choices.label: Contribute with a reduced personal want
Contribute impulsively and ignore your own needs

scenarios.title: Loan Request
scenarios.prompt: Someone close asks to borrow money.

choices.label: Set clear terms or say no kindly
Lend a small amount you can lose
Lend money you cannot afford to lose

weeks.learningGoal: Learn the value of comparison shopping

scenarios.title: Same Item Different Prices
scenarios.prompt: You find the same item at three different prices.

choices.label: Buy the middle option for convenience
Buy the first one you saw

scenarios.title: Brand vs Basic
scenarios.prompt: A name-brand item and a generic version both meet your need.

choices.label: Pick based on price and quality together
Choose the brand for status only

scenarios.title: Quick Purchase Pressure
scenarios.prompt: You feel rushed to buy something before looking around.

choices.label: Buy immediately because you are in a hurry
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

tracksTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

weeks.learningGoal: Understand long-term goals vs short-term spending

scenarios.title: Big Goal Reminder
scenarios.prompt: You remember a big goal you care about while a fun want appears today.

choices.label: Spend a little, save the rest
Forget the goal for today’s fun

scenarios.title: Weekend Temptation
scenarios.prompt: Weekend plans compete with your larger saving target.

choices.label: Set a small budget for the weekend
Spend freely and restart later
choices.summary: Restarts are easy to promise and hard to live.

scenarios.title: Goal Progress Check
scenarios.prompt: You are closer to a goal than before and can choose your next step.

choices.label: Pause the goal for random spending

weeks.learningGoal: See how consistent saving builds security

scenarios.title: Savings Cushion
scenarios.prompt: You notice your savings is giving you breathing room.

scenarios.title: Review Week Win
scenarios.prompt: Your review shows consistent saving across multiple weeks.

choices.label: Use the win as an excuse to splurge
choices.summary: Lets success become the setup for a setback.

scenarios.title: Peace of Mind Choice
scenarios.prompt: You feel less stressed because money is set aside.

choices.label: Protect that peace by staying consistent
choices.summary: Sees saving as emotional strength too.

choices.label: Abandon the plan because things feel better
choices.summary: Comfort can undo the system that created it.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand how interest grows savings

scenarios.title: Savings Growth Notice
scenarios.prompt: You notice your account balance grew a little from interest.

choices.label: Add more to the account
choices.summary: Uses proof to strengthen the habit.

choices.label: Withdraw it because it is extra money

scenarios.title: Interest Lesson
scenarios.prompt: A banker or teacher explains how interest works.

choices.label: Use the lesson to save more intentionally
Listen but make no change yet
choices.summary: Knowledge without action has limited power.

choices.label: Ignore the lesson
choices.summary: Misses an easy tool for growth.

scenarios.title: Small Gain Question
scenarios.prompt: The gain looks small and you wonder if saving is worth it.

choices.label: Stay patient and keep growing it
choices.summary: Understands that little gains build over time.

choices.label: Give up because it is not fast enough

weeks.learningGoal: Learn how high-yield savings accounts work

scenarios.title: HYSA Option
scenarios.prompt: You can move money into a high-yield savings account.

choices.label: Open or use the HYSA
choices.summary: Places money where it can work harder.

choices.label: Research it first and start small

scenarios.title: Birthday Money Deposit
scenarios.prompt: You receive extra money and must choose where to place it.

choices.label: Put it in the HYSA
choices.summary: Turns a bonus into stronger growth.

choices.label: Spend it because it feels separate from the budget

scenarios.title: Growth Comparison
scenarios.prompt: You compare what your money earns in checking versus HYSA.

choices.label: Move only a little for now
Do nothing because it feels like too much effort

weeks.learningGoal: Understand Certificates of Deposit (CDs)

scenarios.title: Six-Month CD
scenarios.prompt: A bank offers a six-month CD with a stronger rate.

choices.label: Put money you will not need into the CD
Keep money in HYSA instead
choices.summary: Still a smart option with more flexibility.

scenarios.title: Locked Money Concern
scenarios.prompt: You worry about not touching money placed in a CD.

choices.label: Avoid all growth tools because of discomfort

scenarios.title: Friend’s Opinion
scenarios.prompt: A friend says CDs are boring and not worth it.

choices.label: Follow your friend and spend instead

weeks.learningGoal: Learn the power of financial patience

scenarios.title: Waiting Pays
scenarios.prompt: You can wait a few weeks for a better option.

choices.label: Take a smaller version now
choices.summary: Mixed, because you still control the damage.

scenarios.title: Goal Almost There
scenarios.prompt: You are close to reaching a goal and feel tempted to stop.

choices.label: Push through and finish strong
choices.summary: Uses patience to complete the mission.

scenarios.title: Sale Countdown
scenarios.prompt: The countdown clock tries to rush you again.

choices.label: Let the deal go if the budget says no
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

choices.label: Research it before the sale ends
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

choices.label: Buy fast because you might miss out
choices.summary: Lets fear of missing out spend your money.
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

tracksTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

weeks.learningGoal: Understand risks of spending future money today

scenarios.title: Spend Next Week’s Money
scenarios.prompt: You think about using money that really belongs to next week.

choices.label: Do not touch next week’s money
Borrow a tiny amount with a payback plan
choices.summary: Risky, but controlled if truly repaired.

choices.label: Use it freely now
choices.summary: Steals from the future version of you.

scenarios.title: Advance Offer
scenarios.prompt: Someone offers an easy pay-later option on something fun.

choices.label: Say no and wait until you can afford it
Use it only for a true need
Use pay-later for a want
choices.summary: Turns tomorrow’s money into today’s toy.

scenarios.title: Borrow From a Goal
scenarios.prompt: You are tempted to borrow from money meant for a larger goal.

choices.label: Borrow a little and replace it quickly
Use the goal money and hope to rebuild later

weeks.learningGoal: Learn good banking habits

scenarios.title: Account Check
scenarios.prompt: You review your accounts and notice where your money is sitting.

choices.label: Make one improvement today
choices.summary: Good step, though not full cleanup.

scenarios.title: Debit Card Habit
scenarios.prompt: Your card makes spending easy and fast.

choices.label: Use it with a small cap
Swipe without checking
choices.summary: Treats bank access like unlimited money.

scenarios.title: Bank Alert Setup
scenarios.prompt: You can turn on alerts for low balance or charges.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand contracts and financial agreements

scenarios.title: Loan to a Friend
scenarios.prompt: Someone asks for money and promises to pay it back later.

choices.label: Set clear terms or decline
choices.summary: Protects the relationship and the money.

scenarios.title: Service Agreement
scenarios.prompt: A service asks you to accept terms before signing up.

choices.label: Skim key parts and ask questions

scenarios.title: Shared Purchase Plan
scenarios.prompt: A group wants to agree on sharing a cost later.

choices.label: Get the agreement in clear terms
Agree casually but write down basics

weeks.learningGoal: Learn to read agreements carefully

scenarios.title: Tiny Print
scenarios.prompt: The small print contains details about fees and timing.

choices.label: Ask someone to explain the key points
Ignore the tiny print
choices.summary: Lets hidden terms ambush you later.

scenarios.title: Auto-Renew Clause
scenarios.prompt: A plan renews automatically unless canceled.

choices.label: Notice it and set a reminder
Notice it but hope you remember
Miss the clause entirely
choices.summary: Passive agreement leads to active charges.

scenarios.title: Return Policy
scenarios.prompt: The return rules are stricter than you expected.

choices.label: Buy only if you are sure
Ignore the policy and buy anyway
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

tracksTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

weeks.learningGoal: Understand payment plans and total cost

scenarios.title: Three Easy Payments
scenarios.prompt: A store offers a payment plan that sounds simple.

choices.label: Use it only for a real need
choices.summary: Mixed, because the tool is still risky.

choices.label: Use it for a want because the payment looks small
choices.summary: Lets the small payment hide the big cost.

scenarios.title: Split-Pay App
scenarios.prompt: A checkout app offers to split the cost into smaller amounts.

choices.label: Pay in full only if budgeted
Use split pay with strict tracking

scenarios.title: Monthly vs Total
scenarios.prompt: A purchase looks affordable monthly but expensive overall.

choices.label: Focus only on the monthly amount

weeks.learningGoal: Recognize too-good-to-be-true offers

scenarios.title: Flash Sale Message
scenarios.prompt: A message says today only and huge savings.

choices.label: Verify the offer first
choices.summary: Treats excitement like a clue, not proof.
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

choices.label: Research quickly before acting
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

tracksTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

scenarios.title: Luxury for Cheap
scenarios.prompt: An item looks far below normal price.

choices.label: Be skeptical and compare sources
choices.summary: Understands that low price can hide risk.

choices.label: Ask questions before buying
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

choices.label: Grab it fast because it is rare

tracksTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

scenarios.title: Urgent Offer
scenarios.prompt: The seller warns that someone else might take it right now.

choices.label: Walk away if pressured
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

tracksTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

weeks.learningGoal: Learn how scams target rushed decisions

scenarios.title: Fake Prize
scenarios.prompt: You get a message saying you won something but must pay first.

choices.label: Verify it with a trusted adult or source
Pay to claim the prize
choices.summary: Lets greed or excitement open the door.

scenarios.title: Suspicious Seller
scenarios.prompt: A seller refuses normal payment methods and wants something unusual.

choices.label: Send money anyway
choices.summary: Gambles with your cash and safety.

scenarios.title: Link Pressure
scenarios.prompt: A text says your account has an issue and asks you to click now.

choices.label: Go to the real app or site directly
Pause and ask someone you trust

weeks.learningGoal: Think long-term before big purchases

scenarios.title: Laptop Choice
scenarios.prompt: You want a bigger purchase that matters to school or goals.

choices.label: Plan and save for it
choices.summary: Makes the purchase purposeful and sustainable.

choices.label: Buy the flashy version without a plan

scenarios.title: Transportation Upgrade
scenarios.prompt: You want a bike or another larger item that helps daily life.

choices.label: Research total cost and save first
choices.summary: Understands that big buys need systems.

choices.label: Rush into the first option
choices.summary: Makes size of purchase bigger than your preparation.

scenarios.title: Special Event Cost
scenarios.prompt: A larger one-time expense appears for something exciting.

choices.label: Plan ahead and set a cap
Go all out because it feels special

weeks.learningGoal: Learn to save for large purchases

scenarios.title: Goal Jar
scenarios.prompt: You are actively saving for a larger purchase.

choices.label: Add to the goal again this week
choices.summary: Builds the purchase the right way.

choices.label: Pause the goal for wants
choices.summary: Lets the big purchase drift away.

scenarios.title: Used vs New
scenarios.prompt: You can save longer for new or reach the goal faster with used.

choices.label: Choose the option that matches your budget and purpose

scenarios.title: Almost There
scenarios.prompt: You are close to the amount needed.

choices.label: Delay the purchase for one more check
Use a pay-later shortcut
choices.summary: Lets impatience tax the finish line.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand goal-based budgeting

scenarios.title: Category Budgeting
scenarios.prompt: You can assign money toward a specific goal this week.

choices.label: Give the goal its own budget line
Do not budget for the goal at all

scenarios.title: Wants Compete With Goal
scenarios.prompt: A want pops up right when the goal contribution is due.

choices.label: Skip the goal for the want

scenarios.title: Visible Progress
scenarios.prompt: Seeing progress makes the goal feel real.

choices.label: Use that momentum to stay consistent
Keep going at the same pace

weeks.learningGoal: Recognize opportunities that require savings

scenarios.title: Class Trip Chance
scenarios.prompt: An opportunity appears that needs money soon.

choices.label: Use saved money and decide calmly
Cover part and ask for time on the rest
Miss it because all money was spent
choices.summary: Teaches the cost of not being ready.

scenarios.title: Discounted Tool or Course
scenarios.prompt: A valuable opportunity appears for something educational or useful.

choices.label: Use savings if it truly matches your goals
Buy it without checking fit or budget
choices.summary: Opportunity without strategy can still be waste.
choices.trackTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

tracksTouched: Too Good To Be True Track

scenarios.title: Community Event Entry
scenarios.prompt: There is a low-cost event that could help your growth.

choices.label: Go because you prepared for things that matter
Adjust the budget carefully to attend
Cannot go because spending was never controlled
choices.summary: Readiness was lost earlier, not today.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand discipline and financial independence

scenarios.title: Routine Over Mood
scenarios.prompt: You do not feel like sticking to the budget today.

choices.label: Drop the rules because you are tired

scenarios.title: Quiet Win
scenarios.prompt: No emergency happened, but discipline still matters.

choices.label: Keep strong habits even without pressure

scenarios.title: Independent Choice
scenarios.prompt: No one is watching your money choices this week.

choices.label: Make the wise choice anyway
choices.summary: Proves the habit is internal now.

choices.label: Spend loosely when no one sees
choices.summary: Shows discipline was external, not owned.

weeks.learningGoal: Balance generosity and responsibility

scenarios.title: Help With Boundaries
scenarios.prompt: Someone needs help, but you also have your own responsibilities.

scenarios.title: Birthday or Celebration
scenarios.prompt: You want to bless someone without overspending.

choices.label: Choose a thoughtful low-cost option
choices.summary: Shows meaning does not require overspending.

scenarios.title: Saying No Kindly
scenarios.prompt: You realize that declining a request can still be respectful.

choices.label: Say no with honesty and care
Say yes when you mean no
choices.summary: Avoids discomfort by creating money stress.

weeks.learningGoal: See how habits build long-term success

scenarios.title: Strong Pattern
scenarios.prompt: You notice multiple good habits are working together.

choices.label: Reward yourself with something small and planned
Assume success means you can stop trying

scenarios.title: System Check
scenarios.prompt: Your routines are making money decisions easier.

choices.label: Simplify one part of the system
Stop tracking because it feels repetitive
choices.summary: Repetition is often the point of a good habit.

scenarios.title: Habit Pressure Test
scenarios.prompt: A stressful week tempts you to abandon your routines.

choices.label: Lean on the routine anyway
choices.summary: Uses habit as support, not a chore.

weeks.learningGoal: Understand how past decisions affect future finances

scenarios.title: Earlier Choice Echo
scenarios.prompt: A current option is shaped by what you did several weeks ago.

choices.label: Use the lesson and choose better now
Recover partially from the earlier choice

scenarios.title: Consequence Review
scenarios.prompt: You can now see how earlier spending affected current flexibility.

choices.label: Adjust based on what you learned
Blame luck only
choices.summary: Misses the role of earlier choices.

scenarios.title: Window of Opportunity
scenarios.prompt: A chance appears and your old habits determine your options.

choices.label: Handle it okay, but not perfectly
Miss the chance because of earlier habits
choices.summary: Today’s miss was built over time.

weeks.learningGoal: Build financial confidence

scenarios.title: Money Voice
scenarios.prompt: You need to speak up about a money issue fairly and calmly.

choices.label: Stay silent even though it costs you

scenarios.title: Smart Choice Without Approval
scenarios.prompt: You make a wise choice others may not understand.

choices.label: Second-guess yourself but still choose well
Change your choice to fit in
choices.summary: Lets outside voices steer your money.

scenarios.title: Banking Question
scenarios.prompt: You do not understand something and can ask or pretend.

choices.label: Pretend to understand and choose badly

weeks.learningGoal: Understand planning reduces financial stress

scenarios.title: Plan vs Panic
scenarios.prompt: Something stressful comes up and you notice your plan helping or not helping.

choices.label: Use the plan and stay calm
React without a plan
choices.summary: Stress grows when structure is missing.

scenarios.title: End-of-Week Feeling
scenarios.prompt: You compare how this week felt to past weeks.

choices.label: See mixed results and tighten the plan
Ignore the lesson and keep winging it
choices.summary: Misses peace that structure could bring.

scenarios.title: Deadline Pressure
scenarios.prompt: A due date or choice feels stressful right now.

choices.label: Spend quickly just to end the feeling
choices.summary: Buys short relief and longer problems.

weeks.learningGoal: Learn how budgeting supports life goals

scenarios.title: Budget for Something Bigger
scenarios.prompt: You see how budgeting could help a life goal you care about.

choices.label: Tie the budget to that goal
Start with one small goal line
Keep budgeting separate from your dreams

scenarios.title: Purposeful Spending
scenarios.prompt: A decision forces you to ask what your money is for.

choices.label: Spend without connecting to any goal
choices.summary: Money drifts where purpose is absent.

scenarios.title: Goal Priority Check
scenarios.prompt: You must choose between a goal expense and a random want.

choices.label: Prioritize the goal expense
choices.summary: Shows budgeting can serve the future you want.

choices.label: Delay the want and revisit later
Pick the want because it feels better today
choices.summary: Makes goals more decorative than real.

weeks.learningGoal: See how money habits compound over time

scenarios.title: Small Wins Add Up
scenarios.prompt: You notice little good habits have created a noticeable difference.

choices.label: Assume the work is done now
choices.summary: Stops the engine that created growth.

scenarios.title: Growth Reflection
scenarios.prompt: Your reflection shows several weeks of smart choices working together.

choices.label: Write down what worked
choices.summary: Turns success into a repeatable system.

choices.label: Notice it but do not record it
Treat the result like luck only
choices.summary: Misses the role of repeated choices.

scenarios.title: Compound Trouble
scenarios.prompt: You also notice repeated bad habits can add up too.

choices.label: Use that insight to tighten up now
choices.summary: Learns from both sides of compounding.

weeks.learningGoal: Reflect on discipline, planning, and patience

scenarios.title: Year Review
scenarios.prompt: You look back at how your choices shaped your year.

choices.label: Reflect honestly and keep the strong habits
choices.summary: Turns one year into future wisdom.

choices.label: Celebrate progress and note one change
Focus only on wins and ignore weak spots

scenarios.title: Next Year Plan
scenarios.prompt: You can decide how to use what you learned next year.

choices.label: Build next year’s plan from this year’s lessons
Keep what worked and adjust later
Start over with no lessons kept

scenarios.title: Identity Statement
scenarios.prompt: You decide what kind of money person you want to be.

choices.label: Choose improvement even if not perfect
Say it does not matter who you become with money
choices.summary: Misses the deeper purpose of the game.
